title: . Rehabilitation services.
content:
. Rehabilitation services. 
a. The System shall, as soon as practicable, establish a continuing program, for the rehabilitation of 
injured and handicapped employees who shall be entitled to rehabilitation services, which shall 
consist of medical, surgical or hospital treatment, including appliances if they have been 
handicapped by the injury, to help them become physically independent. 
   
b. As soon as practicable, the System shall establish centers equipped and staffed to provide a 
balanced program of remedial treatment, vocational assessment and preparation designed to meet 
the individual needs of each handicapped employee to restore him to suitable employment, 
including assistance as may be within its resources, to help each rehabilitee to develop his mental, 
vocational or social potential.  
Chapter VI 
DISABILITY BENEFITS
